description:
CU-LT 200x100 is an optimised rectangular fire damper with a fire resistance up to 120 minutes. It is the smallest type of a fire damper series and has a width of 200 mm and a height of 100 mm. A minimal pressure loss is guaranteed thanks to the thin blade and the transmission located outside the tunnel. A galvanised steel tunnel contributes to the light weight of the damper. The fire damper can be equipped with various types of mechanisms (motorised or not), depending on the specific needs linked to the project or to the local regulations. In this EPD, one specific motorised actuator is considered as a reference.

Fire dampers are installed where air ducts penetrate fire-resistant compartment walls. Their role is to restore the fire resistance grade of the penetrated wall and to prevent smoke propagation. Fire dampers are distinguished by their degree of fire resistance, by their aeraulic properties and by their installation ease. Rf-Technologies solutions are designed and approved for all types of walls: vertical and horizontal, in concrete or lightweight materials. They are compliant with the most stringent local and European standards. Furthermore, the products for the compartmentation and smoke control provide fire safety for commercial and institutional buildings across Europe: office buildings, hospitals, museums, multi-family housing, public buildings and shopping centres.

In general, a CU-LT fire damper consists of three main parts, i.e. a fire damper casing, mainly composed of a galvanised steel duct, a fire damper blade, composed of a 25 mm thick Promatect H plate, and an actuator (motorised or not). In this EPD, a motorised actuator is considered as a reference.

This EPD is a specific EPD for one specific product, i.e. CU-LT 200x100 fire damper with motorised actuator, from one single company, i.e. Rf-Technologies, that is produced in one single site, i.e. Oosterzele, Belgium.
intended_use: Fire dampers are installed where air ducts penetrate fire-resistant compartment walls. Their role is to restore the fire resistance grade of the penetrated wall and to prevent smoke propagation. Fire dampers are distinguished by their degree of fire resistance, by their aeraulic properties and by their installation ease. Rf-Technologies solutions are designed and approved for all types of walls: vertical and horizontal, in concrete or lightweight materials. They are compliant with the most stringent local and European standards. Furthermore, the products for the compartmentation and smoke control provide fire safety for commercial and institutional buildings across Europe: office buildings, hospitals, museums, multi-family housing, public buildings and shopping centres.

reference_flow.description: The functional unit for this EPD is 1 CU-LT 200x100 fire damper with a motorised actuator, to be used in conjunction with partitions to maintain fire compartments in heating, ventilation and air conditioning installations, with a fire resistance up to 120 minutes and a reference service life of 30 years. Packaging of the raw materials and the finished product is included. The net weight per reference flow is 4,856 kg.

geographical_representativity: The CU-LT 200x100 fire damper with motorised actuator is produced in Belgium by the Belgian company Rf-Technologies and is installed in Belgium. The EPD is representative for the Belgian market. The composed datasets for this EPD are representative and relevant for a CU-LT 200x100 fire damper with motorised actuator produced by Rf-Technologies in Belgium.
production_process: For the production of the CU-LT 200x100 fire damper, at first, the parts for the calcium silicate damper blade and the metal tunnel body are produced as sub-assembly parts. These two parts come together in the final assembly line. Here, the following steps are performed: assembly of the damper blade, assembly of the transmission, mounting of the actuator on the damper, adding labels, end control and test. Finally, the finished and tested fire damper is packed.
